# Current Bahamas — Compliance Checklist: Boutique Hospitality
# Version 1.0 | April 2026
# For: Hotels, lodges, villas, guesthouses, boutique resorts (5–60 rooms)
# Derived from master checklist — hospitality-specific items added

---

## Client: ___________________________  Code: ________
## Property type: _____________  Rooms: ____  Island: ________________
## Checklist prepared: _______________  By: ___________

---

## SECTION 1: BUSINESS LICENCE

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Business licence current and correct category (Hotel / Guest House / Rental) | [ ] | |
| Licence covers all revenue-generating activities on property | [ ] | e.g. bar, restaurant, tours, water sports |
| Separate licences for any sub-activities if required | [ ] | |
| Renewal due date in Airtable | [ ] | |
| Financial statements ready if turnover >$250K | [ ] | |

**Hospitality note:** If the property operates a bar, restaurant, or activity offering, check whether each requires a separate licence category.

---

## SECTION 2: VAT

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| VAT registered? | [ ] Yes [ ] No | Required once VAT registration threshold is met |
| VAT applies to: room revenue, F&B, tours, ancillary services | [ ] | Confirm applicable categories with accountant |
| Last VAT return period and date | [ ] | Period: ________ Filed: ________ |
| Seasonal revenue fluctuations documented | [ ] | High season vs shoulder vs off-season |
| VAT filing calendar set — all 12 periods in Airtable | [ ] | |

---

## SECTION 3: NIB

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| NIB employer number confirmed | [ ] | |
| Seasonal staff pattern documented | [ ] | High season hires / off-season reductions |
| Employee count updated at start and end of each season | [ ] | |
| Last NIB payment confirmed current | [ ] | Period: ________ Paid: ________ |
| Any staff classified as contractors — NIB obligations reviewed | [ ] | Contractor vs employee classification matters for NIB |

**Seasonality trigger:** Update employee count in Airtable when seasonal staff are hired or released. NIB must reflect actual headcount.

---

## SECTION 4: TAX CLEARANCE CERTIFICATE (TCC)

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Current TCC on file | [ ] | |
| TCC required for: government grants, Tourism Investment Board dealings, Ministry of Tourism partnerships | [ ] | |
| TCC expiry in Airtable (30-day reminder set) | [ ] | |
| Any government grants anticipated (MSME, Tourism Fund) | [ ] | Detail: |
| TCC readiness status | [ ] Ready [ ] Gap-fill needed [ ] Blocker present | |

---

## SECTION 5: AGRICULTURE PERMITS (FOOD SERVICE PROPERTIES)

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Property operates a restaurant or kitchen? | [ ] Yes [ ] No | If No, skip |
| Agricultural produce imported? | [ ] Yes [ ] No [ ] Confirm | |
| Permit categories required (produce, dairy, meat, poultry, fish) | [ ] | |
| Pre-purchase permit process confirmed with property manager | [ ] | |
| Seasonal ordering calendar aligned with permit lead times | [ ] | |

---

## SECTION 6: EXEMPTIONS — HOSPITALITY SPECIFIC

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| FIDEA eligibility (Family Island properties) | [ ] Yes [ ] No [ ] Assess | |
| Tourism Investment Board incentives assessed | [ ] | |
| Duty concessions on capital equipment (furniture, fixtures, generator, solar) | [ ] | |
| Any planned renovations or capital expenditure in next 12 months | [ ] | Trigger for exemption packaging |
| Existing concessions documented and expiry tracked | [ ] | |

---

## SECTION 7: ADDITIONAL HOSPITALITY ITEMS

| Item | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Health and safety compliance (Ministry of Health inspections) | [ ] | Note: CB tracks but does not advise — refer to operator |
| Liquor licence current (if applicable) | [ ] | |
| Water craft / boat licences current (if applicable) | [ ] | |
| Staff work permits current (foreign national employees) | [ ] | Phase 3 service — log count here |
| Environmental compliance (Bahamas Environment, Science and Technology Commission) | [ ] | |
